title: Zero Waste Life
subTitle: Gardener Yamashita Rikito collects plants with nowhere to go, that must be removed for demolition or renovation, and finds them new homes; saving them while saving those who give them up from regret.

title: Zero Waste Life
subTitle: Gardener Yamashita Rikito collects plants with nowhere to go, that must be removed for demolition or renovation, and finds them new homes; saving them while saving those who give them up from regret.

title: Medical Frontiers
subTitle: Explores the impact of GLP-1 receptor agonists on obesity management, while highlighting the significant risks associated with unsupervised use and the wider therapeutic potential of these treatments.

title: NHK Newsline
subTitle: Hourly news updates featuring current affairs, business news, financial updates and global weather forecasts.

title: A Mile in Your Shoes
subTitle: We look at the efforts of Israeli-American robotics expert Tamir Blum, who develops AI robots that will help Japanese agriculture deal with issues such as labour shortages and the aging population.

title: Trails to Oishii Tokyo Mini
subTitle: Focusing on moyashi, also known as bean sprouts. They may not look like much, but they're packed with vitamins and minerals. They're easy to use, and most importantly, affordable, lending to their popularity.

title: NHK Newsline
subTitle: Hourly news updates featuring current affairs, business news, financial updates and global weather forecasts.

title: Hometown Stories
subTitle: Intimate portraits of people from around Japan, each leading diverse lives while enriching lives of others, with themes ranging from ancient traditions to contemporary social issues.

title: Sumopedia
subTitle: A Sumo wrestling referee's attires hold meaning behind the colourful traditional look and is actually believed that it symbolises the victory.

title: NHK Newsline
subTitle: Hourly news updates featuring current affairs, business news, financial updates and global weather forecasts.

title: Activate Your Japanese
subTitle: Lessons on how the viewer can make the most out of their Japanese language skills. This time, a look at what one can say and do when it is time to change the subject in a social environment.

title: Culture Crossroads
subTitle: Culture Crossroads comprises several regular programs with the theme of creating a multicultural society. They look at international exchange programs and give tips on disaster preparedness.

title: Hello! NHK WORLD-JAPAN
subTitle: March 2026 marks 15 years since the Great East Japan Earthquake. Throughout the month, NHK World-Japan will show programs looking at the present and future of the disaster-hit areas.

title: Newsroom Tokyo
subTitle: Comprehensive coverage of events in Japan and Asia. It explains the day's developments and what is behind them.

title: Direct Talk
subTitle: Murase Hidehiro trains search-and-rescue dogs to save lives in disaster areas around the world. He talks about the importance of having dogs that are specifically able to respond to disasters in Asia.

title: NHK World Selection
subTitle: A selection of acclaimed programmes plucked straight from NHK's domestic catalogue of channels dubbed into English.

title: NHK World Japan
subTitle: A dapper young man sits in a teahouse with his older companion when a young woman catches his eye. From the way they're looking at each other, it's clear that there's something going on between them.

title: A Second Disaster: 15 Years After the Tsunami
subTitle: In a town nearly destroyed by the 2011 tsunami, fishermen who helped rebuild now face a second disaster as climate change and warmer seas threaten their future.

subTitle: This program visits places across Japan to introduce the charms of their local daily life and festivals fostered by the nation's long history.
